name: Iowa Speech-Language Pathologist License
description: Already licensed as a speech-language pathologist? Here's whether your license transfers to Iowa - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Board of Speech Pathology and Audiology
provider.parentOrganization: Iowa Department of Inspections, Appeals and Licensing

address.description:
Iowa Department of Health and Human Services
Professional Licensure
321 East 12th Street
Des Moines, IA 50319-0075

requiredDocuments.name: Praxis exam score report
requiredDocuments.description: Score of 162 or higher on test 5331.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does an Iowa SLP license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: The total initial cost is approximately $120. Biennial renewal is $120.

mainEntity.name: Is Iowa part of the ASLP Interstate Compact?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Iowa has enacted ASLP-IC legislation.

mainEntity.name: Does Iowa allow telepractice for SLPs?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Iowa permits telepractice for licensed SLPs and compact privilege holders.

mainEntity.name: Does Iowa have reciprocity for speech-language pathologists?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. Iowa participates in the ASLP-IC. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in Iowa without a separate endorsement application.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my speech-language pathologist license to Iowa?

acceptedAnswer.text: Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Iowa while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
